description: Make targeted device sends reliably auto-deliver through the existing relay-server plus SSE architecture, with explicit receiver presence, delivery state, fallback-to-queue behavior, and clear cross-platform status UI.

userStories.title: Server: track targeted delivery lifecycle state
userStories.description: As a developer, I need persisted delivery lifecycle state for targeted clips so the product can distinguish notification, delivery, and fallback instead of treating them as the same event.
acceptanceCriteria: Add persisted delivery state for targeted clips covering at least targeted_pending, targeted_delivered, targeted_fallback, and failed
A targeted clip is not marked delivered only because an SSE event was emitted
Untargeted clip behavior remains unchanged
go test ./... passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Server: expose receiver connectivity on devices list
userStories.description: As a sender, I want each device to report whether its auto-receive channel is actually online so I can tell whether targeted delivery is likely to complete automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/v1/devices includes receiver status for each device
Receiver status distinguishes at least online, degraded, and offline
Online status is derived from an active and fresh SSE subscription for that device
Server tests cover connect, disconnect, and stale-heartbeat transitions
go test ./... passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Server: confirm targeted delivery on successful raw consume
userStories.description: As a receiving client, I need the server to treat successful raw consumption by the addressed device as the delivery confirmation so targeted delivery has one authoritative success condition.
acceptanceCriteria: Successful GET /api/v1/clips/:id/raw by the addressed device marks the targeted clip as delivered
Duplicate or wrong-device consume attempts do not mark the clip delivered
Handler comments and API behavior document raw consume as the targeted delivery acknowledgement
go test ./... passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Server: fallback targeted clips back into recoverable queue state
userStories.description: As a user, I want targeted clips that fail auto-delivery to remain available for manual receive instead of disappearing silently.
acceptanceCriteria: If a targeted clip is not consumed within a configurable timeout after notification, it moves to targeted_fallback
Fallback clips remain visible to the target device in the queue
Queue responses distinguish fallback items from normal untargeted queue items
Server tests cover timeout-triggered fallback and later manual receive
go test ./... passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Server: add targeted delivery diagnostics and regression coverage
userStories.description: As a developer, I need logs and tests around targeted auto-delivery so failures can be diagnosed and regressions caught quickly.
acceptanceCriteria: Server logs targeted clip creation, SSE notify, raw consume success, fallback trigger, and expiry
Tests cover online delivery, replay after reconnect, fallback after timeout, and duplicate consume returning 410 Gone
go test ./... passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: macOS: surface self receiver status and SSE health
userStories.description: As a macOS user, I want to see whether this device is currently ready for targeted auto-delivery so I can trust the direct-send behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Config or main panel shows self receiver status: connected, reconnecting, or disconnected
Status is driven by the real SSE connection state rather than static config presence
Reconnect backoff state is visible while SSE is retrying
swift build succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: macOS: show target device receiver readiness in picker
userStories.description: As a macOS sender, I want the target device picker to show whether the selected destination is online for automatic delivery before I send content.
acceptanceCriteria: Target device picker shows receiver status for each device returned by GET /api/v1/devices
Offline or degraded targets are visually distinct from online targets
Sending to an offline or degraded target warns that the item may fall back to queue
swift build succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: macOS: make targeted auto-receive failure visible and recoverable
userStories.description: As a macOS receiver, I want failed targeted auto-delivery to tell me the item is still recoverable from the queue instead of failing silently.
acceptanceCriteria: On targeted auto-receive failure, the app shows a non-intrusive warning that the item remains in queue
Queue UI marks targeted fallback items distinctly from normal queue items
Successful text and image targeted receives still update NSPasteboard and file receives still save to Downloads
swift build succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Windows: surface self receiver status and SSE health
userStories.description: As a Windows user, I want to see whether this device is actively connected for targeted auto-delivery so I can trust direct sends.
acceptanceCriteria: Main or config UI shows self receiver status: connected, reconnecting, or disconnected
Status reflects live SSE loop state and reconnect attempts
dotnet build succeeds on a Windows host
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Windows: show target device receiver readiness and fallback messaging
userStories.description: As a Windows sender, I want target selection and send feedback to explain whether automatic delivery is expected or whether the clip will fall back to queue.
acceptanceCriteria: Target device selector shows receiver status for each candidate device
Sending to an offline or degraded target warns about queue fallback
Queue rows distinguish fallback items from normal queue items
dotnet build succeeds on a Windows host
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Windows: make targeted auto-receive failure visible and recoverable
userStories.description: As a Windows receiver, I want failed targeted auto-delivery to surface clear fallback status instead of silently doing nothing.
acceptanceCriteria: On targeted auto-receive failure, the app shows a toast or inline warning that the item remains in queue
Successful targeted text and image receives still update Clipboard and file receives still save to Downloads
SSE disconnect and reconnect events are logged for diagnostics
dotnet build succeeds on a Windows host
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Android: expose foreground receiver status for targeted auto-delivery
userStories.description: As an Android user, I want the app to indicate whether the foreground service is currently able to auto-receive targeted clips so I know whether direct delivery is armed.
acceptanceCriteria: Foreground service or settings UI exposes receiver status based on active SSE health
Status distinguishes connected, reconnecting, and unavailable states
./gradlew assembleDebug succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Android: preserve queue fallback when targeted auto-delivery fails
userStories.description: As an Android receiver, I want targeted clips to stay manually recoverable if auto-delivery fails because the service, auth, or connectivity is not healthy.
acceptanceCriteria: On targeted auto-delivery failure, the app shows that the clip remains available in queue
Queue UI distinguishes fallback items from normal queue items
Successful targeted text receives still update ClipboardManager and file receives still save to Downloads
./gradlew assembleDebug succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Shared UX: label queue mode versus targeted auto-delivery mode
userStories.description: As a user, I want the product to clearly distinguish queue send from targeted auto-delivery so the behavior is understandable before and after each send.
acceptanceCriteria: UI labels queue mode and targeted auto-delivery mode explicitly across macOS, Windows, and Android
Send feedback distinguishes delivered automatically, waiting for target device, and delivered to queue fallback
Queue UI marks fallback items distinctly from untargeted queue items
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes
